LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JASON MERTZ-PRICKETT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AT ANY STAGE IN A CASE. AND NONE OF THE DEFENDANTS HAVE IDENTIFIED ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE TO THEM IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED. EVEN IF MR. MERTZ-PRICKETT WAS NOT AS DILIGENT IN SEEKING TO AMEND AS HE MIGHT HAVE BEEN, NO DEFENDANT HAS IDENTIFIED ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE TO HIM OR IT IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED. BECAUSE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ADD TWO PARAGRAPHS TO AN EXISTING CAUSE OF ACTION AND NOT AN ENTIRELY NEW CAUSE OF ACTION, THE ARGUMENT THAT THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT DOESN'T STATE A VALID CLAIM LACKS MERIT AND, IN ALL EVENTS, DEFENDANTS ARE NOT FORECLOSED FROM FILING A DEMURRER TO THE IIED CLAIM, ALTHOUGH DOING SO IS HIGHLY UNLIKELY TO BE EFFICACIOUS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2018 Set For Trial On Jan-14-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Jason Mertz-Prickett's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage in a case. and none of the defendants have identified any legal prejudice to them if the motion is granted. Even if Mr. Mertz-Prickett was not as diligent in seeking to amend as he might have been, no defendant has identified any legal prejudice to him or it if the motion is granted. Because the amended complaint seeks to add two paragraphs to an existing cause of action and not an entirely new cause of action, the argument that the proposed amendment doesn't state a valid claim lacks merit and, in all events, defendants are not foreclosed from filing a demurrer to the IIED claim, although doing so is highly unlikely to be efficacious. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Berman, Esq. 650-843-1900 of Pierce & Shearer for Plaintiff Jason Mertz-Prickett.
Stephanie Yang, Esq. 415-394-9400 of Jackson Lewis for Defendant CHRISTOPHER BRUNO, MCKESSON EMPLOYEES FEDERAL CREDIT UNION.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues cause of action 8 and 9. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER BRUNO, MCKESSON EMPLOYEES FEDERAL CREDIT UNION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained without leave to amend in part.

The Court rules on Defendants' demurrers to the following causes of action:

First (Penal Code 528.5): OVERRULED; the statutory language - e.g., 528.5(a) - is broad enough to encompass the alleged conduct; it is not limited to "creation of a fake account."

Second (Penal Code 502): OVERRULED; the statutory language is broad enough to encompass the alleged conduct. The statute defines "data" expansively and Plaintiff may be able to prove he had an ownership interest in, e.g., his login credentials.

Third (Invasion of Privacy): OVERRULED; Plaintiff has adequately pled illicit use by Defendants of his logon credentials; whether such illicit use was allowed by company policy goes to the merits and is not susceptible to demurrer.

Fourth (Labor Code 1102.5 Retaliation): OVERRULED; the statute applies to employers "or any person acting on behalf of the employer." The California Supreme Court decision Defendants rely on is from 2008, before Labor Code 1102.5 was amended to add the cited language. Defendants do not contend that any California appellate court has found individual liability unavailable under the amended statute. Given that statute's plain language, I do not agree with a San Diego federal judge's prediction that our Supreme Court would find no individual liability. (See Lupo v. Quality Assur. Servs., Inc. (S.D. Cal. 2017) 242 F.Supp.3d 1020.) The better course is to permit this cause of action to proceed.

Seventh (False Light): SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. "When an action for libel is alleged, a false-light claim based on the same facts'is superfluous and should be dismissed." (McClatchy Newspapers, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1987) 189 Cal.App.3d 961, 965.)

Eighth (Defamation): OVERRULED; sufficient facts are pled. Demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored; "ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.)

Ninth (Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress): OVERRULED; the alleged conduct is sufficiently outrageous, and using an employee's login credentials to commit fraud, as alleged, is outside the scope of normal employment relations and thus not pre-empted by workers compensation.

Tenth (Civil Conspiracy): OVERRULED; several of the torts alleged provide predicates for a civil conspiracy claim.

Twelfth (Tort of Another): OVERRULED; Plaintiff has alleged several torts by Defendants that resulted in him having to respond to CUMIS' denial of bondability.

Thirteenth (Declaratory Relief): OVERRULED; a possibility of future wrongs exists. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840, mlyon@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)
